# Solar — Full API Reference

> A runtime-based JavaScript UI framework designed for AI-generated code. No build step. Explicit prop contracts, structured errors, and a machine-readable component registry.

Solar runs entirely in the browser via CDN or npm. Every component declares a typed prop schema. Violations throw structured `ContractError` objects before the component renders. The global registry exposes `manifest()` — a JSON string of every registered component and its schema, designed to be passed directly to an AI agent as context.

---

## Installation

### CDN (no install)

```html
<script type="module">
  import { defineComponent, mountComponent, useState, createElement }
    from 'https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/solarbuild/framework/index.js'
</script>
```

### npm

```bash
npm install solarbuild
```

```js
import { defineComponent, mountComponent, useState, createElement } from 'solarbuild'
```

### Scaffold

```bash
npm create solarbuild@latest my-app
cd my-app && npm run dev
```

---

## Project structure

```
my-app/
├── index.html
├── main.js           # mounts root components
└── components/
    ├── Button.js     # one component per file
    ├── Counter.js    # file name matches component name
    └── UserCard.js   # default export is always defineComponent(...)
```

---

## defineComponent(config)

The core primitive. Defines a component with a typed prop schema and render function.

```js
import { defineComponent, createElement, registry } from 'solarbuild'

const Button = defineComponent({
  name: 'Button',
  props: {
    label:   { type: 'string',   required: true },
    onClick: { type: 'function', required: true },
    variant: { type: 'string',   enum: ['primary', 'secondary'], default: 'primary' },
  },
  render({ label, onClick, variant }) {
    return createElement('button', { class: variant, onclick: onClick }, label)
  },
})

registry.register(Button)
export default Button
```

### Config

| Field    | Type     | Required | Description |
|----------|----------|----------|-------------|
| `name`   | string   | yes      | Unique component name. Used in errors, registry, slot validation |
| `props`  | object   | no       | Schema of accepted props |
| `render` | function | yes      | Receives resolved props, returns a vnode |

### Prop descriptor fields

| Field      | Type    | Description |
|------------|---------|-------------|
| `type`     | string  | `string`, `number`, `boolean`, `function`, `object`, `array`, `any`, `slot` |
| `required` | boolean | Throws if missing or null |
| `default`  | any     | Value used when prop is not passed |
| `enum`     | array   | Restricts value to a list of allowed strings |
| `accepts`  | string  | For `slot` type only — restricts to a specific component name |

### Types helper

```js
import { Types } from 'solarbuild'
Types.string   // 'string'
Types.number   // 'number'
Types.boolean  // 'boolean'
Types.function // 'function'
Types.object   // 'object'
Types.array    // 'array'
Types.any      // 'any'
Types.slot     // 'slot'
```

### Return value

`defineComponent` returns a component function. Call it with props to get a vnode:

```js
Button({ label: 'Save', onClick: handleSave, variant: 'primary' })
```

### Slots (typed composition)

```js
const Card = defineComponent({
  name: 'Card',
  props: {
    title:  { type: 'string', required: true },
    action: { type: 'slot',   accepts: 'Button' }, // must be a Button vnode
  },
  render({ title, action }) {
    return createElement('div', { class: 'card' },
      createElement('h2', {}, title),
      action,
    )
  },
})

// valid
Card({ title: 'Hello', action: Button({ label: 'Go', onClick: () => {} }) })

// throws ContractError — plain createElement vnode has no _source tag
Card({ title: 'Hello', action: createElement('button', {}, 'Go') })
```

---

## ContractError

When a prop contract is violated, Solar throws a `ContractError` immediately — before the component renders.

```json
{
  "error": "ContractError",
  "component": "Button",
  "prop": "label",
  "expected": "string",
  "received": "number",
  "fix": "Pass a string value for \"label\"",
  "message": "Button: prop \"label\": expected string, got number"
}
```

Use `e.toJSON()` for the structured object. Use `e.message` for the human-readable string.

### All ContractError scenarios

| Scenario | `expected` | `fix` |
|----------|-----------|-------|
| Required prop missing | `"a value (required)"` | Provide a value for the required prop |
| Wrong type | e.g. `"string"` | Pass the correct type |
| Invalid enum value | `"one of [\"primary\", \"secondary\"]"` | Use one of the allowed values |
| Slot prop got a non-object | `"a vnode (slot)"` | Pass a rendered component vnode via `h()` |
| Slot prop from wrong component | `"slot(Icon)"` | Pass a vnode produced by the required component |
| Array expected | `"array"` | Pass an array |
| `registry.register()` called with plain function | `"a component created with defineComponent()"` | Wrap with `defineComponent()` before registering |
| `mountComponent()` called with plain function | `"a component created with defineComponent()"` | Wrap with `defineComponent()` before mounting |
| `mountComponent()` called before `registry.register()` | `"component to be registered via registry.register()"` | Call `registry.register(Fn)` before mounting |

### Plain hook errors (not ContractError)

These throw a standard `Error` when a hook is called outside a component render:

- `useState called outside of a component render`
- `useMemo called outside of a component render`
- `useResource called outside of a component render`
- `useSubscription called outside of a component render`
- `onMount called outside of a component render`
- `onUnmount called outside of a component render`
- `useRoute called outside of a component render`

### defineComponent errors

- `defineComponent: "name" is required` — `name` field was omitted
- `defineComponent(Button): "render" is required` — `render` function was omitted

### Console warnings (non-throwing)

- `Button: unknown prop "colour" passed but not declared in schema` — prop not in schema, often a typo

---

## mountComponent(component, props, el, depth?, options?)

Renders a component into a DOM node.

```js
import { mountComponent } from 'solarbuild'
import Button from './components/Button.js'

mountComponent(Button, {
  label: 'Click me',
  onClick: () => console.log('clicked'),
}, document.getElementById('app'))
```

### onMount option

For code that runs after mount but lives outside a component:

```js
mountComponent(App, {}, document.getElementById('app'), 0, {
  onMount: () => {
    const canvas = document.querySelector('canvas')
    startAnimation(canvas)
  }
})
```

---

## Hooks

All hooks must be called inside a component's `render` function — never conditionally, never outside a component.

### useState(initial)

```js
const [count, setCount] = useState(0)

setCount(5)                  // set directly
setCount(c => c + 1)         // set from previous value (preferred for derived updates)
```

Setter skips re-render if the new value is strictly equal to the previous.

Lazy initialization — pass a function to compute the initial value once:

```js
const [data, setData] = useState(() => JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem('cache')))
```

**Returns:** `[value, setter]`

---

### useMemo(compute, deps)

Caches an expensive computation. Recomputes only when deps change.

```js
const sorted = useMemo(
  () => items.slice().sort((a, b) => a.name.localeCompare(b.name)),
  [items]
)
```

`deps` can be an array or plain object — compared by value, not reference.

**Returns:** the cached value.

---

### useResource({ key, fetch })

Fetches async data. Re-fetches when `key` changes. Automatically cancels the previous in-flight request.

```js
const { data, loading, error } = useResource({
  key: userId,
  fetch: async (signal) => {
    const res = await fetch(`/api/user/${userId}`, { signal })
    return res.json()
  },
})

if (loading) return createElement('p', {}, 'Loading...')
if (error)   return createElement('p', {}, error.message)
return createElement('p', {}, data.name)
```

| Field     | Type    | Description |
|-----------|---------|-------------|
| `data`    | any     | Resolved value from the last successful fetch, or null on first load |
| `loading` | boolean | True while a fetch is in flight |
| `error`   | Error\|null | Set if the fetch threw, null otherwise |

Previous data remains available during a key transition — `data` is not cleared to null.

---

### useSubscription({ source, event, handler })

Attaches an event listener and removes it on unmount or when any parameter changes.

```js
const [width, setWidth] = useState(window.innerWidth)

useSubscription({
  source:  window,
  event:   'resize',
  handler: () => setWidth(window.innerWidth),
})
```

Works with DOM `EventTarget`s (`.addEventListener`) and Node-style emitters (`.on`).

---

### onMount(fn)

Runs once after the component's first render. Not called on re-renders.

```js
onMount(() => {
  analytics.track('component_mounted')
})
```

---

### onUnmount(fn)

Runs once when the component is removed from the DOM.

```js
onUnmount(() => {
  subscription.cancel()
  clearInterval(timer)
})
```

---

### useRoute()

Returns the current hash route inside a component.

```js
import { useRoute } from 'solarbuild'

const PostPage = defineComponent({
  name: 'PostPage',
  props: {},
  render() {
    const { path, params } = useRoute()
    return h(['h1', {}, `Post ${params.id} at ${path}`])
  },
})
```

**Returns:** `{ path, params }` — `path` is the current hash path (e.g. `'/post/42'`), `params` is an object of captured `:param` values.

---

## h(node) — Compact notation

Array-based alternative to `createElement`. Easier to generate programmatically.

```js
import { h } from 'solarbuild'

h(['div', { class: 'card' },
  ['h2', {}, 'Title'],
  ['p',  {}, 'Body'],
])
```

Each node: `[type, props?, ...children]`

- `type` — tag name string (`'div'`) or registered component name (`'Button'`)
- `props` — optional object of attributes or props
- `children` — nested arrays or primitives

### Registry dispatch

If `type` matches a registered component name, `h()` calls that component — prop validation still runs:

```js
h(['Button', { label: 'Save', onClick: handleSave, variant: 'primary' }])
// equivalent to: Button({ label: 'Save', onClick: handleSave, variant: 'primary' })
```

### Special props

**style** — object (preferred) or string:

```js
h(['div', { style: { backgroundColor: '#09080a', padding: '2rem' } }])
h(['div', { style: 'background: #09080a' }])
```

**innerHTML** — set raw HTML; children are ignored when present:

```js
h(['div', { innerHTML: '<span class="keyword">const</span> x = 1' }])
```

---

## Registry

Central store of all registered components.

```js
import { registry } from 'solarbuild'
```

### registry.register(component)

```js
registry.register(Button)
```

Throws if the argument was not created with `defineComponent`.

### registry.get(name)

```js
const Button = registry.get('Button')  // component function or undefined
```

### registry.has(name)

```js
if (registry.has('Button')) { ... }
```

### registry.list()

Returns an array of all components with their schemas:

```js
registry.list()
// [{ name: 'Button', props: { label: { type: 'string', required: true }, ... } }]
```

### registry.manifest()

Returns `registry.list()` serialized as a JSON string. Pass this to an AI agent as context — the agent can read it and know exactly what components exist and how to call them:

```js
const context = registry.manifest()
// pass as part of a system prompt, tool result, or MCP resource
```

Example output:

```json
[
  {
    "name": "Button",
    "props": {
      "label":   { "type": "string",   "required": true },
      "onClick": { "type": "function", "required": true },
      "variant": { "type": "string",   "enum": ["primary", "secondary"], "default": "primary" }
    }
  }
]
```

---

## Router

Hash-based router. No server config needed — works on any static host.

```js
import { createRouter } from 'solarbuild'

const router = createRouter({
  '/':         HomePage,
  '/about':    AboutPage,
  '/post/:id': PostPage,
  '*':         NotFound,   // 404 fallback — must be last
})

router.mount(document.getElementById('app'))
```

### Route patterns

| Pattern       | Matches                          |
|---------------|----------------------------------|
| `'/'`         | `#/` or no hash                  |
| `'/about'`    | `#/about`                        |
| `'/post/:id'` | `#/post/123` → `params.id = '123'` |
| `'*'`         | anything unmatched               |

### router.navigate(path) / navigate(path)

```js
import { navigate } from 'solarbuild'
navigate('/post/42')
```

### Route components receive params as props

```js
const PostPage = defineComponent({
  name: 'PostPage',
  props: { id: { type: 'string', required: true } },
  render({ id }) {
    return h(['h1', {}, `Post ${id}`])
  },
})
```

---

## mountPortal(component, props, targetEl)

Renders a component into any DOM node — useful for modals, toasts, overlays:

```js
import { mountPortal } from 'solarbuild'

mountPortal(Modal, { title: 'Confirm', onClose: () => {} }, document.body)
```

---

## Agent usage pattern

### 1. Discover components

```js
const manifest = registry.manifest()
// pass to your agent as system context — it describes every component and prop schema
```

### 2. Mount with error handling

```js
import { registry, mountComponent, ContractError } from 'solarbuild'

function agentMount(componentName, props, container) {
  const fn = registry.get(componentName)
  if (!fn) throw new Error(`No component named "${componentName}". Available: ${registry.list().map(c => c.name).join(', ')}`)

  try {
    return { id: mountComponent(fn, props, container) }
  } catch (e) {
    if (e instanceof ContractError) return { error: e.toJSON() }
    throw e
  }
}
```

### 3. Self-correction loop

When a mount returns `{ error }`, feed `error.fix` back to the agent with a retry prompt. The `fix` field is a human-readable correction the agent can act on directly.

### Notes for agents

- Always call `registry.manifest()` before generating props — never assume prop names or types.
- `function` props cannot be serialized to JSON. Use a fixed set of named handlers exposed by the host app, or have the agent generate handler code as a string for the runtime to evaluate.
- Components must be registered before mounting. If `registry.get(name)` returns `undefined`, the component is not registered.
- `unmountComponent(id)` tears down hooks, child instances, and removes the DOM node.

---

## Full minimal app

```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<body>
  <div id="app"></div>
  <script type="module">
    import {
      defineComponent, mountComponent,
      useState, createElement, registry
    } from 'https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/solarbuild/framework/index.js'

    const Counter = defineComponent({
      name: 'Counter',
      props: {
        label: { type: 'string', default: 'Count' },
      },
      render({ label }) {
        const [count, setCount] = useState(0)
        return createElement('div', {},
          createElement('p', {}, `${label}: ${count}`),
          createElement('button', { onclick: () => setCount(c => c + 1) }, '+'),
        )
      },
    })

    registry.register(Counter)
    mountComponent(Counter, { label: 'Score' }, document.getElementById('app'))
  </script>
</body>
</html>
```

---

## Links

- Docs: https://docs.solarbuild.dev
- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/solarbuild
- GitHub: https://github.com/Stahlwalker/framework-solar
